docs.title: keeplog
docs.text:
Terminal session logger with full-text search.

Records every command + output in your terminal, saves to SQLite, and lets you search instantly.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• PTY capture — records full output, not just commands
  • SQLite + FTS5 — instant full-text search
  • fzf integration — interactive fuzzy search with preview
  • Zero config — install and forget
  • Two modes — full (capture output) or light (metadata only)
  • Cross-platform — macOS + Linux, zsh + bash

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Description record Start recording session setup Add auto-start hook to shell rc remove Remove auto-start hook search <query> Interactive search (fzf if available) recent Show recent commands get <id> Show full command details + output status Show stats last Show last session export Export all data as JSON clear <days> Clear data older than N days config [key val] Get/set configuration init Initialize database

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
# Set mode to light (metadata only, no output)
keeplog config mode light

# Set retention to 7 days
keeplog config retention_days 7

# View current config
keeplog config

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Key Default Description mode full Capture mode (full or light) retention_days 30 Auto-delete data older than this

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/rathinadev/keeplog
cd keeplog
pip install -e .
python -m pytest tests/

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
src/keeplog/
├── cli.py              # CLI dispatcher
├── db.py               # SQLite + FTS5 storage
├── capture.py          # PTY capture loop
├── install.py          # Auto-start setup
├── search.py           # fzf integration
├── config.py           # Config file
└── hooks/
    ├── bash.sh
    └── zsh.sh

docs.title: Record a Session
docs.text:
keeplog record

This spawns your shell inside a pseudo-terminal and records everything. Type commands normally, then exit when done.

docs.title: Search
docs.text:
# Interactive fzf search
keeplog search docker

# Without fzf, it falls back to a list
keeplog search "npm install"

docs.title: Browse
docs.text:
# Recent commands
keeplog recent

# Full details of a specific command
keeplog get 1

docs.title: Auto-start
docs.text:
keeplog setup

Adds a hook to your .zshrc/.bashrc so every terminal starts recording automatically.
